page: Amazon Web Services c8ine.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 virtual CPUs and 16 physical Intel cores with a dedicated allocation, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This hardware profile is engineered specifically for compute-intensive workloads requiring dedicated processor resources without local storage overhead.

Network performance is a key characteristic of this instance, which delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. It is optimized for both network throughput and Elastic Block Store integration, though it does not include any local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it a highly specialized option for workloads that rely entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ine.8xlarge offers a balanced profile for high-throughput network tasks and compute-heavy operations by eliminating the cost overhead of local disks and graphics hardware. This makes it suitable for workloads such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, and network-bound application servers that require consistent CPU performance and high network capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ine.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical Intel cores) running at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU accelerators, focusing instead on high-speed network and EBS performance with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for compute-heavy and network-intensive workloads that utilize network-attached storage. It is well-suited for applications such as batch processing, distributed computing, and high-throughput web serving where dedicated CPU resources and high network capacity are required without the added cost of local storage or specialized GPU hardware.
